cover.title: THE GEM
cover.subtitle: The one who sees what could be refined, and says it before the heart is visible.
cover.tagline: You don't point things out because you don't care. You point them out because you see what they could become.

profile_scores.caption: High drive and expression, low warmth in delivery. You see the spot that could get better, and you say it exactly as it is.

short_summary: You see the last part that could make something better, and you rarely pretend not to see it.
The loneliness comes when people hear criticism, even when what you meant was potential.
What changes next is not becoming less precise. It's letting your heart arrive with the precision.

full_report.headline: THE GEM
full_report.intro: You are someone who says what you see. If something could get better but stops right there, you don't just let it go. You point it out precisely. This report follows the pattern underneath that: why your refinement is often misread as criticism, why that makes people shrink back, and how your care can be heard more accurately.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're someone who says what you see. If something could get better but stops right there, you don't just let it go. You point it out precisely.
People call you sharp because of that. They call you picky too. Sometimes they call you cold.
What people often miss is this. You are not searching for flaws. You notice the part that could become even better.
It's the same when you love. When you see that just a little more polish would make things so much better, you say it right away. Because you see the potential.
But the person hearing it may only hear, "I'm not enough again." You may not be angry at all. You may simply be disappointed because you can see what could be possible.
chapters.profile_note: High Drive · High Expression, Low Warmth in Delivery. You see the spot that could get better, and you say it exactly as it is.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Repeat: THE NEVER ENOUGH CURVE
body: Early in a relationship, people may trust you because your words feel honest and there is little empty praise.
Later, the same precision can become harder to hear. You are still noticing what could improve, but the other person may hear only what is missing.
They may not feel that you are seeing potential. They may feel that they are being measured and coming up short.
So they may start trying less. You notice that too. But what looks like a loss of effort can sometimes be discouragement.
You saw the change accurately, but the feeling underneath it may have been harder to read.
chapters.highlight: The loneliness is not that you care about precision. It is that the care behind it does not always arrive with the words.

chart.title: THE NEVER ENOUGH CURVE
chart.description: The pattern where, as the relationship deepens, your wish to refine things further stays the same, the other person's attempts keep shrinking, and you keep precisely pointing out that shrinking.

chapters.title: What You Got Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: That is part of it. But you may also see refinement as a form of care. The other person may not know how to hear that precision as affection.
items.truth_short: You may see refinement as care, while others hear only criticism.

items.belief: You should listen to what's true.
items.truth: Truth matters. So do timing and delivery. Being right and being helpful in that moment are not always the same thing.
items.truth_short: Truth matters, but timing and delivery shape how it lands.

items.belief: If I let it slide, I lose myself.
items.truth: You do not have to see less or lose your standards. What helps is making the care behind your precision visible too.
items.truth_short: You can keep your precision and still show the care behind it.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: Different people can bring out the same feeling in you.
You may be drawn to someone who settles for "good enough," even when you can clearly see more potential.
That can feel frustrating, not because they lack ability, but because you can see the final step they are choosing not to take.
chapters.highlight: Someone who cares about you may still find your precision hard to hear. A healthier bond makes room for both your standards and their dignity.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need is not to become less perceptive.
What helps is adding the heart behind what you noticed.
Do not stop at what feels unfinished. Say what you see in them too, and why the detail matters to you.
That extra context can help the difference between criticism and care become easier to hear.
chapters.action_sentence: Let the other person hear both what could improve and why you care.

chapters.title: Where to Go From Here
body: You already know how to spot the place that could get better. You know how to say it accurately.
But what you'll learn next is how to show that your gaze comes from seeing potential.
This is not about becoming less precise.
It is about helping your sincerity arrive with the precision.
chapters.transition_note: It is about helping your sincerity arrive with the precision.

chapters.title: The Type You Keep Falling For

items.type_title: Someone Who Stops at Good Enough
items.description: The project is almost finished, and they say, "That's enough." You immediately see the final part that could make it stronger. That unfinished edge keeps pulling at you.

items.type_title: Someone Who Chooses Comfort Over Growth
items.description: When things get difficult, they may ask why life needs to be harder. You can understand the logic and still feel sad about the growth they keep leaving behind.

items.type_title: Someone Who Thinks Close Enough Is Fine
items.description: They shrug and say, "It's basically the same thing." For you, the difference between almost and exact can matter. For them, it may not.

items.type_title: Someone Who Notices Only the Big Picture
items.description: They notice the whole picture but miss the tiny adjustments you care about. You know small details can change the result, but not everyone feels that difference the same way.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: Some of these signs may have shown up early. Your answer was often to explain your point more precisely.

signal_strength.label: After hearing what you point out, the other person's words gradually shrink
You may read their quieter response as an effort to improve
They begin trying less because the process starts to feel discouraging
You notice the drop in effort but may miss the discouragement underneath it

chapters.core_signal: The other person may hear anger or judgment when what you are actually feeling is disappointment and care.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like to You

checklist.title: They understand the heart behind your gaze.
checklist.description: They can hear the care behind your attention to detail, even when the words are difficult.

checklist.title: They don't fall apart hearing something true.
checklist.description: They can stay in the conversation without treating every correction as rejection.

checklist.title: They take precision as affection.
checklist.description: Your precision is not mistaken for a lack of affection.

checklist.title: Being together makes things more refined.
checklist.description: This matters. Both people can help each other grow without one person becoming the constant evaluator.

chapters.title: What Happens in Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You're vulnerable to someone who hears every suggestion as proof that they are not enough. They may begin talking less and trying less, while you keep noticing the distance. Without a safer way to communicate, both of you can end up feeling misunderstood.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier match is someone who can ask what you mean instead of assuming the worst, and someone who can tell you when your delivery hurts. With that kind of honesty, you do not have to keep explaining your heart after every sentence.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to someone whose pace or standards are different from yours. That does not decide the outcome. What matters is whether you can show the care behind your precision, and whether they can respond without shrinking or shutting down.
sections.next_signs: This time, the pattern may show up in small changes. Their answers get shorter after feedback. They stop sharing early drafts. Notice whether the two of you can talk about that distance before it grows.
sections.try_this: When you point something out, include what you value and what you hope for. Then ask how your words landed. Make room for their response instead of deciding from one moment whether they can handle your honesty.
sections.final_line: What matters is whether precision and care can be heard together, over time.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to see what could be refined. What changes now is making sure the care behind that precision is visible too.
action_items: After pointing something out, add what you value and why the detail matters to you.
Ask how your words landed instead of judging the relationship from one response.
Before repeating the same point, check whether the other person feels discouraged or shut out.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning to see and say where things could get better. The next report covers why your precision has so often been misread as criticism, and how you can deliver the heart behind it more accurately.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
